description:
## Parent

bsm-7en — Render selected issue details in the issue explorer

## Background and intent

Beadwork Issues may include comments with discussion, links, code, and lists. After the shared safe Markdown renderer exists for descriptions, the Issue Detail can reuse that policy to show comments read-only. This gives users the rest of the Issue context without turning Beadsmith into an editor or discussion client.

Comments must remain presentation-only in this epic. Creating, editing, deleting, replying, or syncing comment mutations are all out of scope. The goal is simply to make existing Beadwork comments readable when present and invisible when absent.

## What to build

Render a Comments section in the selected Issue Detail only when the selected Issue has comments. For each comment:

- show the raw timestamp
- show author only when present
- render the comment body as Markdown through the shared `MarkdownContent` component from bsm-7en.3

The Markdown behavior for comments must match descriptions: GFM support, raw HTML disabled, external HTTP(S) links opened outside the app through the safe opener seam, and all other link-like values rendered inert.

## Reasoning and constraints

- Reuse the shared Markdown renderer. Do not create a separate comment-specific Markdown implementation.
- Keep comments read-only. Do not add inputs, reply buttons, edit affordances, delete affordances, or mutation RPCs.
- Omit the Comments section entirely when `comments` is empty so the pane focuses on available content.
- Display timestamps as raw strings. Do not add formatting rules in this epic.
- Keep author optional; missing/empty author should not create placeholder clutter.

## Testing guidance

Component tests should cover the section-level omission behavior and representative comment rendering. MarkdownContent should already have broad renderer tests from bsm-7en.3, so comment tests can focus on integration and avoid duplicating every Markdown case unless needed to prove the comment body uses the shared renderer.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Issue Detail renders a Comments section only when the selected Issue has comments.
- [ ] Issues with no comments omit the Comments section entirely.
- [ ] Each rendered comment shows its raw timestamp.
- [ ] Comment author is shown only when present.
- [ ] Comment body renders through the shared `MarkdownContent` component with the same safe Markdown/link behavior as descriptions.
- [ ] No comment creation, editing, deletion, reply, or mutation behavior is added.
- [ ] Component tests cover absent comments, author display, missing-author omission, timestamp display, Markdown comment rendering, and safe link behavior in comment bodies where not already covered by MarkdownContent tests.
- [ ] Existing description Markdown behavior from bsm-7en.3 continues to pass.

## Out of scope

- No comment creation, editing, deletion, or reply behavior.
- No comment mutation RPCs.
- No formatted/relative timestamp display.
- No dependency navigation.
- No E2E expansion in this bead.

## Blocked by

bsm-7en.3
title: Render read-only Markdown Issue comments
